calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Alexander Perrotti (via Zoom).
Michael Cashman for petitioner and objector Stephen P. Ganley, who was also present.
Stacey Nelson for beneficiary KQED (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed; and 2) Petition for Letters of Administration.

The Court stated it had reviewed the filings and prior proceedings. The Court found that the decedent executed a valid will in 2021 and a valid codicil. The Court further noted that a prior objection had been overruled and found that the subsequently filed objection did not constitute a proper will contest.

After hearing argument, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated January 22, 2021, and codicil dated October 2024, were admitted to Probate. Debra Dolch was appointed administrator with will annexed, with full IAEA authority. Bond was fixed at $1,816,000. Frank Mahoney III was appointed probate referee.

The Court set a status hearing regarding the filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration for September 15, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and stated that if either petition is filed at least two weeks prior to the status date, the hearing will be taken off calendar.

The competing petition was denied.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Continued To Mar-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Continued To Mar-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Alexander Perrotti (via Zoom).
Michael Cashman for objector Stephen Ganley, who was also present.
Travis Ganley, interested party.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

Mr. Cashman stated that a competing petition and objections had been submitted for filing and that he had recently been retained by Mr. Ganley. The Court stated that it could not review or consider documents that had not yet been processed through the Court's e-filing system, noting that doing so could constitute an improper ex parte communication.

Mr. Wrobel requested that the Court grant the petition and appoint Debra Dolch as administrator with will annexed, and stated that objections previously raised by Mr. Ganley had been overruled at a prior hearing on November 12, 2025. Mr. Cashman stated that Mr. Ganley was unrepresented at the time of the prior hearing and requested that the Court allow review of the newly submitted filings now that Mr. Ganley is represented.

The Court stated that it wished to proceed on a clean record and declined to rule while filings were pending and unavailable for review.

The Court continued the matter to March 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Continued To Jan-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Continued To Dec-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Alexander Perrotti (via Zoom).
Stephen Ganley, objector in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

Mr. Wrobel confirmed that the petition sought admission of the will dated January 22, 2021, and codicil dated October 2024, which together modified both the will and the decedent's trust to name KQED and the San Francisco SPCA as beneficiaries.

Discussion was held regarding the objection filed by Stephen Ganley. Mr. Wrobel argued that it did not raise valid grounds to oppose admission of the will and that many of the issues raised concerned the Richard and Marie Ganley Trust, which was not before the Court. He noted that the decedent's trust currently lacked an acting trustee and that a professional fiduciary might be necessary to administer the estate and related trust assets.

Mr. Ganley alleged misconduct by the decedent relating to their parents' estate and contended that property in Las Vegas and other assets were improperly removed from their parents' trust and should not be included in Lorna Ganley's estate. The Court advised Mr. Ganley that those matters were beyond the scope of the current petition and suggested he seek legal counsel or contact the Court's Access Center for assistance.

The Court overruled Mr. Ganley's objection and found it did not constitute an objection to the will or codicil. The Court indicated that a licensed professional fiduciary should be appointed as administrator and directed Mr. Wrobel to identify one. The matter was continued to December 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Continued To Nov-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thoams Wrobel appeared via zoom for Alexander Perrotti (petitioner).
Steven Ganley appeared in person pro per (objector).

Mr. Ganley stated that he contested what's going on in the redistribution of his father's wealth, that he is in touch with counsel and getting advice on how to move forward, and that he will retain counsel soon.

Mr. Wrobel stated that the proposed administrator used to work at his law firm but now is a Law school, that it should not be a conflict of interest, and that he occasionally helps out at his law firm.

Mr. Ganley is to file his written objections by October 29, 2025.

The matter is continued to November 12, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Oct-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
